behavioral_properties.name: Trust-First Synchronization
behavioral_properties.description: First authenticated message counter from unsynchronized peer is trusted
behavioral_properties.issue_description: Trust-first synchronization is susceptible to accepting a replayed message after a Node has been rebooted
behavioral_properties.affected_scenario: Node reboot without persisting Group Peer State Table
behavioral_properties.mitigation: Use cache-and-sync policy for security-sensitive applications; control messages accept higher risk
behavioral_properties.impact: Potential replay attack if peer state not persisted across reboot

behavioral_properties.name: Cache-and-Sync Synchronization
behavioral_properties.description: Message is stored and sync exchange initiated before processing
behavioral_properties.security_benefit: Replay protection even after node reboot
behavioral_properties.issue_description: Large groups may cause significant message synchronization burden on sender and message queueing pressure on receiver
triggering_scenarios: First message to new large group
Large scale synchronizations at single point in time
behavioral_properties.impact: Network congestion, receiver latency, potential message loss due to queue overflow

behavioral_properties.name: Control Messages Always Trust-First
behavioral_properties.description: All control messages (C Flag = 1) SHALL use trust-first synchronization exclusively
behavioral_properties.applies_to: All control messages regardless of GroupKeySecurityPolicy
behavioral_properties.rationale: Control messages are assumed to be critical and time-sensitive
behavioral_properties.issue: CONSISTENCY ISSUE - Control Message Counter Tracking
behavioral_properties.issue_description: Protocol maintains separate Control Message Counter per peer but never synchronizes it via MCSP (always trust-first)
behavioral_properties.implication: Control Message Counter is metadata artifact with no MCSP synchronization requirement

behavioral_properties.name: At-Most-One Exchange Per Peer-Key Pair
behavioral_properties.description: For each peer Node ID and group key pair there SHALL be at most one synchronization exchange outstanding at a time
behavioral_properties.violation_handling: Subsequent unsynchronized messages during active exchange are either discarded or queued (implementation choice)
behavioral_properties.issue_description: Protocol allows implementation to either discard or queue messages during active MCSP, creating inconsistent behavior
behavioral_properties.impact: Message loss possible if implementation chooses discard; potential queue exhaustion if implementation queues

behavioral_properties.name: Message Ordering Guarantee
behavioral_properties.description: If multiple messages are queued from synchronized peer, they SHALL be processed in order received
behavioral_properties.scope: Same peer, same operational group key
behavioral_properties.criticality: FIFO ordering is essential for application correctness

security_properties.name: Replay Attack Protection via Counter Synchronization
security_properties.description: MCSP prevents replay attacks by ensuring receiver knows sender's current message counter
security_properties.mechanism: Synchronized Data Message Counter + Message Reception State bitmap
security_properties.window_mechanism: MSG_COUNTER_WINDOW_SIZE bitmap allows bounded out-of-order arrivals
security_properties.duplicate_detection: Messages outside counter window are rejected as duplicates
security_properties.weakness: Only protects data messages; control messages use weaker trust-first
security_properties.issue_description: Data messages get full MCSP protection while control messages only get trust-first protection
security_properties.attacker_scenario: If control messages carry sensitive commands, replay vulnerability exists
security_properties.mitigation: Applications should not rely on replay protection for control messages

security_properties.name: Challenge-Response Freshness
security_properties.description: 64-bit random Challenge from DRBG ensures response freshness
security_properties.mechanism: Crypto_DRBG(len = 64 bits) generates unique Challenge per request
security_properties.exchange_binding: Exchange ID and Challenge together prevent response replay
security_properties.strength: 64-bit random makes challenge collision probability negligible (2^64 attempts)

security_properties.name: Authentication via Group Key
security_properties.description: MsgCounterSyncReq and MsgCounterSyncRsp authenticated with operational group key
security_properties.mechanism: Group key encryption with C Flag = 1 (control message counter)
security_properties.assumption: Group key securely shared between all group members
security_properties.limitation: Does not prevent authenticated members from sending invalid responses

security_properties.name: Message Integrity
security_properties.description: Group key provides both encryption and message integrity check
security_properties.coverage: All MCSP message fields (headers and payload)

security_properties.name: Counter Confidentiality
security_properties.description: Synchronized Counter transmitted in encrypted MsgCounterSyncRsp
security_properties.protection: Prevents passive eavesdropper from learning peer counter state
security_properties.limitation: Responder must already know peer identity (source of sync request)

security_properties.name: Destination Node ID Binding
security_properties.description: Response Destination Node ID must match initiator's Node ID
security_properties.mechanism: Destination Node ID verification in T8 guard

security_properties.name: Exchange Isolation
security_properties.description: Each MCSP exchange isolated by unique Exchange ID
security_properties.mechanism: Exchange ID must match between request and response
security_properties.prevents: Cross-exchange confusion and response injection attacks
security_properties.uniqueness: Exchange ID scoped to (Fabric Index, Peer Node ID, Role)

security_properties.name: Source Identity Verification
security_properties.description: Source Node ID of response must match Destination of request

security_properties.name: Fabric Isolation
security_properties.description: Synchronization scoped to specific fabric via Fabric Index
security_properties.mechanism: Fabric Index in session context; operational group key tied to fabric

security_properties.name: DoS Resistance - Single Exchange Per Peer
security_properties.description: At most one synchronization exchange per peer-key pair
security_properties.protection: Prevents resource exhaustion from synchronization flood
security_properties.limitation: No rate-limiting on number of distinct peers

timeout_and_timing_properties.name: Jitter Delay for Thundering Herd Prevention
timeout_and_timing_properties.description: Initiator waits uniformly random time between 0 and MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_REQ_JITTER before sending request
timeout_and_timing_properties.purpose: Prevents multiple receivers from sending sync requests simultaneously after multicast message
timeout_and_timing_properties.issue_description: Protocol does not specify value of MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_REQ_JITTER
timeout_and_timing_properties.impact: Implementations may choose inappropriate jitter values (too small = thundering herd, too large = excessive latency)

timeout_and_timing_properties.name: Timeout for Synchronization Exchange
timeout_and_timing_properties.description: Initiator arms timer to enforce synchronization response within MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT
timeout_and_timing_properties.purpose: Prevents indefinite waiting for response; ensures bounded resource commitment
timeout_and_timing_properties.timeout_action: On expiration: close exchange, discard cached message, peer remains unsynchronized
timeout_and_timing_properties.issue_description: Protocol does not specify value of MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT
timeout_and_timing_properties.impact: Too short timeout = frequent exchange failures; too long = resource exhaustion; inconsistent implementations

timeout_and_timing_properties.name: Counter Window Size
timeout_and_timing_properties.description: Message Reception State bitmap tracks recent window of message counters
timeout_and_timing_properties.purpose: Allows limited out-of-order message arrivals due to network effects
timeout_and_timing_properties.issue_description: Protocol does not specify value of MSG_COUNTER_WINDOW_SIZE
timeout_and_timing_properties.impact: Too small = legitimate out-of-order messages rejected; too large = excessive memory usage; inconsistent window handling
timeout_and_timing_properties.reference: Defined as constant in Section 4.6.5.1 but no value given

state_management_properties.name: Group Peer State Table
state_management_properties.description: Stores information about every peer with which node had group message exchange

required_per_peer_data.synchronized_data_counter: largest encrypted data message counter received
required_per_peer_data.data_counter_synchronized_flag: indicates if counter value is valid and synchronized
required_per_peer_data.data_mrs_bitmap: tracks recent window of data message counters
required_per_peer_data.synchronized_control_counter: largest encrypted control message counter received
required_per_peer_data.control_counter_synchronized_flag: indicates if counter value is valid and synchronized
required_per_peer_data.control_mrs_bitmap: tracks recent window of control message counters

state_management_properties.name: Synchronization Session Context
state_management_properties.description: Context maintained while conducting MCSP exchange with peer
state_management_properties.initiator_lifetime: Maintained throughout full exchange of request and response messages
state_management_properties.responder_lifetime: Maintained only long enough to generate and transmit response message
state_management_properties.unique_key: (Fabric Index, Peer Node ID, Role) comprise unique key for matching incoming messages to ongoing exchanges

state_management_properties.name: Challenge Storage
state_management_properties.description: Challenge value generated in request must be stored and verified in response
state_management_properties.lifetime: From MsgCounterSyncReq transmission until MsgCounterSyncRsp verification or timeout
state_management_properties.verification: Response.Challenge must equal stored Challenge value
state_management_properties.risk: If Challenge not stored securely, response verification can be bypassed

message_structure_properties.name: MsgCounterSyncReq Header Requirements
message_structure_properties.description: Request message header must set specific flags
message_structure_properties.session_id: Group Session ID for operational group key
message_structure_properties.destination_node_id: Peer's Source Node ID (from triggering message)

message_structure_properties.name: MsgCounterSyncReq Payload
message_structure_properties.encryption: Secured with group key for which synchronization is requested
message_structure_properties.counter: Control message counter used for protection

message_structure_properties.name: MsgCounterSyncRsp Header Requirements
message_structure_properties.description: Response message header must set specific flags
message_structure_properties.session_id: Group Session ID for operational group key (from request)

message_structure_properties.name: MsgCounterSyncRsp Payload
message_structure_properties.description: Response message payload contains Synchronized Counter and Response

Synchronized_Counter.value: Current Global Group Encrypted Data Message Counter of responder
Synchronized_Counter.purpose: Informs initiator of sender's current counter value

Response.value: Echo of Challenge from corresponding request
Response.purpose: Bind response to specific request; prevent response replay

transport_and_network_properties.name: Unicast UDP Transport
transport_and_network_properties.description: Both MsgCounterSyncReq and MsgCounterSyncRsp sent over UDP
transport_and_network_properties.issue_description: No retransmission mechanism defined for lost requests or responses
transport_and_network_properties.impact: Network packet loss causes synchronization failure; peer remains unsynchronized until timeout
transport_and_network_properties.network_conditions: Affects high-loss networks (WiFi, lossy links) more than wired networks

transport_and_network_properties.name: IPv6 Requirement
transport_and_network_properties.description: Synchronization messages sent to IPv6 unicast address
transport_and_network_properties.protocol_requirement: IPv6 mandatory; no IPv4 fallback specified

key_and_cryptography_properties.name: Operational Group Key Requirement
key_and_cryptography_properties.description: MCSP synchronization requires pre-shared operational group key
key_and_cryptography_properties.requirement: Group key SHALL be known/derivable by both initiator and responder
key_and_cryptography_properties.assumption: Key has been securely shared before first group message
key_and_cryptography_properties.limitation: Cannot synchronize with peers who don't have operational group key
key_and_cryptography_properties.bootstrap_problem: Protocol doesn't address how group key is initially established

key_and_cryptography_properties.name: DRBG Usage
key_and_cryptography_properties.assumption: DRBG implementation is correctly seeded and provides full entropy

key_and_cryptography_properties.name: Counter Width
key_and_cryptography_properties.rollover_handling: Message counter window calculation uses mod 2^32 arithmetic
key_and_cryptography_properties.warning: After 2^32 messages from same peer, counter wraps to 0; old messages with counter 0 become indistinguishable

consistency_and_correctness_properties.name: Response Reception State Update
consistency_and_correctness_properties.description: Upon successful verification, Message Reception State bitmap is cleared
consistency_and_correctness_properties.action: Clear the bitmap for the peer
consistency_and_correctness_properties.purpose: Reset window tracking after synchronization; next messages evaluated against new counter
consistency_and_correctness_properties.rationale: Old counter window no longer valid after counter update

consistency_and_correctness_properties.name: Silent Failure on Verification
consistency_and_correctness_properties.description: Invalid MsgCounterSyncRsp message is silently ignored
consistency_and_correctness_properties.behavior: No error signal, no state change, timer remains armed
consistency_and_correctness_properties.rationale: Invalid response might be attack; timeout mechanism recovers
consistency_and_correctness_properties.consequence: Legitimate but misrouted responses are dropped silently

consistency_and_correctness_properties.name: Cached Message Processing Order
consistency_and_correctness_properties.description: Multiple cached messages from same peer processed in order received
consistency_and_correctness_properties.scope: Same peer, same operational group key

deployment_and_configuration_properties.name: GroupKeySecurityPolicy Per-Key Configuration
deployment_and_configuration_properties.description: Security policy (trust-first vs cache-and-sync) is configurable per group key
deployment_and_configuration_properties.configuration: via GroupKeySecurityPolicy field in Group Key Management cluster
deployment_and_configuration_properties.flexibility: Different policies for different group keys
deployment_and_configuration_properties.trade-off: Security vs latency trade-off per application

deployment_and_configuration_properties.name: Optional MCSP Support
deployment_and_configuration_properties.description: Cache-and-sync policy and MCSP support is optional
deployment_and_configuration_properties.consequence: Some nodes might not support cache-and-sync
deployment_and_configuration_properties.implication: Large groups with mixed capabilities degrade to trust-first

deployment_and_configuration_properties.name: Peer State Table Persistence
deployment_and_configuration_properties.description: Persistence of Group Peer State Table is not required
deployment_and_configuration_properties.consequence: Node reboot causes all peer state to be lost
deployment_and_configuration_properties.implication: First message after reboot triggers synchronization (cache-and-sync) or trust-first

security_issues.title: Replay Attack After Node Reboot (Trust-First Policy)
security_issues.description: Trust-first synchronization is susceptible to accepting a replayed message after node reboot
security_issues.trigger_condition: Node reboots without persisting Group Peer State Table
security_issues.attack_scenario: After reboot, node has no knowledge of previous max_message_counter for peer P. Attacker replays old message from P with counter=X (within old window). Since node has no MRS state, it treats this as new message and establishes new counter window starting at X. Subsequent legitimate messages with counter > X are accepted.
affected_components: Trust-first synchronization (data messages with trust-first policy)
security_issues.mitigation_in_protocol: Cache-and-sync policy provides protection by synchronizing counter before processing
security_issues.application_responsibility: Applications should use cache-and-sync for security-critical data; accept trust-first risk for non-critical data (e.g., lighting)
security_issues.residual_risk: ACCEPTABLE per spec if application understands trade-off

security_issues.title: Asymmetric Security: Data vs Control Message Protection
security_issues.description: Control messages (C Flag=1) always use trust-first while data messages can use cache-and-sync
security_issues.attack_scenario: If control messages carry critical commands (e.g., 'unlock door'), replay attack after reboot succeeds. If data messages carry non-critical data (e.g., 'room temperature'), application uses cache-and-sync for protection.
security_issues.implication: Protocol design assumes control messages are less security-critical than data, which may not match application requirements
security_issues.affected_commands: All control messages in group communication
security_issues.mitigation_available: None in protocol; applications must implement additional authentication for critical control messages
security_issues.recommendation: Applications should not rely on MCSP for replay protection of critical control commands

critical_issues.title: MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_REQ_JITTER Not Specified
critical_issues.description: Protocol requires jitter delay but does not specify value or recommended range
consequences: Too small (e.g., 0-10ms): Thundering herd problem not prevented; all receivers send requests simultaneously
Too large (e.g., 0-10s): Excessive latency in synchronization; poor user experience
Inconsistent implementations: Different vendors use different values
critical_issues.network_impact: Thundering herd causes network congestion when multicast to large group
critical_issues.protocol_text: When a synchronization request is triggered by an incoming multicast message, the Node SHALL first wait for a uniformly random amount of time between 0 and MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_REQ_JITTER.
critical_issues.missing_guidance: No definition of MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_REQ_JITTER value or range
critical_issues.recommendation: Protocol should specify value (e.g., 100ms) or algorithm (e.g., random(0, 100ms)) or calculation method

critical_issues.title: MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT Not Specified
critical_issues.description: Protocol requires timeout enforcement but does not specify timeout value
critical_issues.impact: Implementations have no guidance on appropriate timeout duration
consequences: Too short (e.g., 100ms): Legitimate responses timeout on slow links; high synchronization failure rate
Too long (e.g., 60s): Resource exhaustion; pending exchanges consume memory; poor responsiveness
Network dependent: Different networks require different timeouts
critical_issues.resource_impact: Session context and Challenge storage duration depends on timeout value
critical_issues.protocol_text: Arm a timer to enforce that a synchronization response is received before MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT.
critical_issues.missing_guidance: No definition of MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT value or recommendation
critical_issues.reference_issue: Initiator must maintain session context 'throughout the full exchange' - duration undefined
critical_issues.recommendation: Protocol should specify value (e.g., 5 seconds) or provide calculation method (e.g., 5 * RTT)

critical_issues.title: MSG_COUNTER_WINDOW_SIZE Not Specified
critical_issues.description: Protocol defines Message Reception State bitmap but does not specify bitmap size
critical_issues.impact: Implementations have no guidance on acceptable out-of-order message window
consequences: Too small (e.g., 8 bits): Out-of-order messages rejected; fragile to network reordering
Too large (e.g., 10000 bits): Memory expensive; bitmap per peer multiplied by number of peers
Inconsistent window sizes: Different implementations accept different message orders
critical_issues.memory_impact: Total MRS memory = (number_of_peers) * (MSG_COUNTER_WINDOW_SIZE bits) * (2 counters: data + control)
critical_issues.protocol_text: The offset into the bitmap equates to the difference between the corresponding message counter and the max message counter... A message counter is within the range of the bitmap, also known as the message counter window, when the counter value is between [(max_message_counter - MSG_COUNTER_WINDOW_SIZE) to (max_message_counter - 1) mod 2^32].
critical_issues.missing_guidance: No definition of MSG_COUNTER_WINDOW_SIZE value or recommended range
critical_issues.recommendation: Protocol should specify value (e.g., 64 or 128 bits) with justification for out-of-order tolerance

consistency_issues.title: Control Message Counter Never Synchronized
consistency_issues.description: Protocol maintains Synchronized Control Message Counter per peer but MCSP never synchronizes control counters
consistency_issues.reason: All control messages use trust-first policy (Section 4.18.1.1), not cache-and-sync
consistency_issues.implication: Control Message Counter is metadata artifact with no functional role in MCSP
consistency_issues.protocol_statement: All control messages (any message with C Flag set) use the control message counter and SHALL use Trust-first for synchronization. Note that MCSP is not used for Trust-first synchronization.
consistency_issues.consequence: Group Peer State Table stores control counter but it's updated via trust-first (Section 4.18.4 step 2), not MCSP
consistency_issues.clarity: Protocol distinguishes control and data counters in state table but this distinction is moot for MCSP
consistency_issues.recommendation: Documentation should clarify that MCSP only synchronizes DATA counters; control counters are separate state

consistency_issues.title: Optional Message Queueing During Active MCSP
consistency_issues.description: Protocol allows implementation to either discard or queue unsynchronized messages arriving during active MCSP exchange
consistency_issues.protocol_statement: Otherwise, do not process the message. An implementation MAY queue the message for later processing after MCSP completes if resources allow.
consistency_issues.scenario: Two unsynchronized messages arrive from same peer during MCSP exchange: Implementation A discards second message (message loss). Implementation B queues second message (resource consumption).
consistency_issues.implication: Applications cannot rely on predictable behavior
consistency_issues.recommendation: Protocol should mandate one behavior or require explicit configuration and documentation

operational_risks.title: Large Group Scalability Concern
operational_risks.description: Large groups may cause significant message synchronization burden on sender and queueing pressure on receiver
triggering_scenarios: Brown-out event: All receivers simultaneously lose power and reboot; all request synchronization at wake-up
New large group formation: All new members request counter synchronization
Large-scale synchronizations at single point in time
operational_risks.network_impact: Sender receives flood of MsgCounterSyncReq messages; response rate limited by sender's processing capacity
operational_risks.receiver_impact: Queue of cached messages waiting for synchronization; potential queue overflow and message loss
operational_risks.performance_degradation: All receivers experience latency spike until synchronization backlog clears
operational_risks.protocol_text: Large groups may cause significant message synchronization burden on the sender and message queueing pressure or latency on the receiver when message counter synchronization state is out of sync, such as after a brown-out, on first message to a new large group, or similar large scale sets of synchronizations being required at a single point in time.
operational_risks.mitigation_in_protocol: Jitter delay (MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_REQ_JITTER) spreads requests over time
operational_risks.mitigation_effectiveness: Limited; jitter reduces peak but doesn't eliminate problem for truly large groups
operational_risks.recommendation: Applications should monitor synchronization performance and implement adaptive policies for large groups

operational_risks.title: UDP Packet Loss No Retransmission
operational_risks.description: MCSP messages sent via UDP; no retransmission or guaranteed delivery
operational_risks.scenario: MsgCounterSyncReq packet lost in network: Initiator waits MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT before giving up; peer never sends response
operational_risks.consequence: Synchronization fails; peer remains unsynchronized; cached message discarded; application doesn't receive data
operational_risks.network_conditions_affected: High-loss wireless networks, congested links, lossy last-mile connections
operational_risks.timeout_trade-off: Short timeout = frequent failures on lossy networks; long timeout = excessive latency on good networks
operational_risks.protocol_text: Invoke Section 4.7.1... to encrypt and then send the request message over UDP to the IPv6 unicast address of the destination.
operational_risks.no_retry_mechanism: Protocol does not define retry logic
operational_risks.comparison: Some protocols (e.g., TCP, CoAP) provide retransmission; MCSP relies solely on timeout
operational_risks.recommendation: Applications on lossy networks should consider cache-and-sync timeout carefully; larger timeout improves reliability but increases latency

operational_risks.title: Peer State Loss After Device Reboot
operational_risks.description: Persistence of Group Peer State Table is not required by protocol
operational_risks.consequence: Device reboot causes all peer counters and MRS bitmaps to be lost
operational_risks.impact: First message from any peer after reboot is treated as unsynchronized
operational_risks.recovery_latency: Device must re-synchronize with all active peers
operational_risks.synchronization_cost: One MCSP exchange per unique peer sending first post-reboot message
operational_risks.network_load: If many peers send simultaneously after reboot, causes thundering herd
operational_risks.protocol_text: NOTE It is not required to persist the Peer State Table.
operational_risks.implementation_choice: Implementations may persist (recommended) or not persist (simpler implementation)
operational_risks.trade-off: Persistence = larger flash/storage footprint; non-persistence = higher post-reboot synchronization load
operational_risks.recommendation: Critical applications should implement persistence; non-critical applications may skip persistence for simplicity

assumption_and_dependency_issues.title: Group Key Pre-Sharing Assumption
assumption_and_dependency_issues.statement: MCSP requires operational group key to be known by both initiator and responder
assumption_and_dependency_issues.implication: Cannot establish group communication with peers who don't have group key
assumption_and_dependency_issues.bootstrap_problem: Protocol doesn't address how initial group key is distributed
assumption_and_dependency_issues.dependency: Relies on separate group key provisioning mechanism (Section 11.2 Group Key Management Cluster)

assumption_and_dependency_issues.title: Secure DRBG Implementation
assumption_and_dependency_issues.statement: Challenge generation assumes Crypto_DRBG is properly implemented and seeded with sufficient entropy
assumption_and_dependency_issues.consequence: Weak DRBG implementation compromises challenge-response security; responses become predictable/replayable
assumption_and_dependency_issues.dependency: Platform must provide cryptographically secure DRBG

assumption_and_dependency_issues.title: Secure Group Key Encryption
assumption_and_dependency_issues.statement: MCSP assumes group key encryption provides both confidentiality and integrity
assumption_and_dependency_issues.dependency: Delegated to Section 4.7.1 (Message Transmission) and Section 4.8.2 (Security Processing)

assumption_and_dependency_issues.title: Atomic Counter Update
assumption_and_dependency_issues.statement: Upon verification success, counter must be atomically updated and MRS bitmap atomically cleared
assumption_and_dependency_issues.consequence: If update is not atomic and message arrives between counter update and bitmap clear, message may be incorrectly handled
assumption_and_dependency_issues.requirement: Implementation must ensure update atomicity or use locking

actionable_findings.action: SPECIFICATION ISSUE: Define MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_REQ_JITTER value or algorithm
actionable_findings.impact: Prevents thundering herd and ensures consistent behavior across implementations

actionable_findings.action: SPECIFICATION ISSUE: Define MSG_COUNTER_SYNC_TIMEOUT value or calculation method
actionable_findings.impact: Prevents resource exhaustion and ensures reasonable latency expectations
Prevents memory explosion and defines acceptable out-of-order message tolerance

actionable_findings.action: SECURITY DOCUMENTATION: Clarify that control messages get lower security than data messages
actionable_findings.impact: Prevents security architecture misunderstandings in applications

actionable_findings.action: OPERATIONAL GUIDANCE: Document large group deployment strategies
actionable_findings.impact: Reduces deployment surprises in production IoT networks

actionable_findings.action: CLARIFICATION: Document that MCSP only synchronizes DATA counters, not control counters
actionable_findings.impact: Improves protocol understanding and implementation correctness

summary.note: Three critical missing constants (JITTER, TIMEOUT, WINDOW_SIZE) mean implementations cannot fully comply with protocol intent; each implementation must make educated guesses about values
